metadata.note: Archetype A (target-centric): primary_target=ASIA-SOUTHKOREA. Scope is the US-ROK alliance and its modernisation -- OPCON transfer, USFK posture, extended deterrence, the nuclear-powered submarine bid, burden-sharing. The ROK centroid carries ~1,055 titles of Samsung/chips/tariff/shipbuilding noise in 180d, so alliance vocabulary must stay institutional. Bare 'submarine' EXCLUDED: it resolves to Hanwha's export bids (Canada, Greece, Poland), which is industrial competition, not deterrence -- 'nuclear-powered' carries the bid instead.
metadata.audit_2026_07_16: The domain verbs are KEPT deliberately, per spec section 2 archetype A: the auditor does not apply primary_target and so overstates their leak. Verified with the gate applied, 'alliance' is 236 -> 19 titles (US-ROK alliance: OPCON, USFK, RIMPAC, ambassador nominee), 'deterrence' -> 4, 'military exercise' -> 7, 'joint drill' -> 4, THAAD -> 9. ja 米韓 kept: 100% nominal foreign but the samples are exactly on-topic (米韓合同軍事演習), the co-tag is Ukraine. DROPPED: ja 国連軍 (rule 6b collision -- substring of 国連軍縮, UN disarmament, which is what it actually matched); ru учени (collision -- substring of ученый/ученик); ru атомн (Iran/Israel-dominated, and gated it would pull ROK civil nuclear-power news); en 'troop level' (generic -- matched US troop cuts in Germany).

bundle.term: US basing geography in the ROK (Pillar 2)
